message: fix(btrfs): reject short compressed extent decode (bd-id7f7)

Cover inspect_image, parse_ext4, parse_btrfs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:17:39.170641522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:57:12Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:57:12Z","close_reason":"Extended fuzz_detect_filesystem to cover inspect_image plus deterministic parse_ext4/parse_btrfs facade consistency checks, then verified the target with remote cargo check and clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-iabzy","title":"Freeze exact FfsError display contracts for mode-violation and repair-failed tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T06:28:00.040908786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T06:35:04.555652859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T06:35:04.553518973Z","close_reason":"Froze exact FfsError display contracts for mode-violation and repair-failed tests and validated with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-error","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-id7f7","title":"Reject short or malformed btrfs compressed extent decode","description":"Fresh-eyes btrfs compression follow-up: btrfs_decompress returns whatever each codec produced without enforcing the file-extent ram_bytes contract, and the LZO segment parser breaks out on malformed segment lengths instead of returning corruption. This can let checksum/format-invalid compressed extents expose partial data or hide malformed tails when the caller only reads an early slice. Acceptance: all ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD decode paths return FfsError::Corruption unless decoded length equals ram_bytes; LZO framing rejects truncated/overrun segments instead of silently breaking; focused tests cover valid LZO framing plus short-output and malformed-frame rejection; parity/spec docs mention exact decoded-length validation; targeted btrfs decompression tests plus fmt/diff/UBS/workspace check/clippy pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:26:21.270141784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:34:28.047078990Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:34:28.044651092Z","close_reason":"Enforced exact ram_bytes decoded-length validation for btrfs ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD extents, made malformed LZO frame parsing return corruption, added focused decoder tests, and updated parity/spec docs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-idp4","title":"Update CHANGELOG.md to cover commits from 2026-03-21 through current","description":"## What
Update CHANGELOG.md to document all significant commits since the last entry (2026-03-21).
Approximately 60+ commits need documentation, organized by capability area (not chronological).

## Why
CHANGELOG.md covers "2026-02-09 through 2026-03-21" with 536 commits documented. Since then,
major work has landed: btrfs experimental RW support, crash-consistency matrix, fallocate
operations, log contract hardening, per-core FUSE dispatch, evidence pipeline expansion,
tracing flake fixes, and 81+ new tests across 4 crates.

The CHANGELOG serves as the project history for onboarding and as input to the
comprehensive-codebase-report skill. Stale changelog = invisible work.

## Implementation Plan
1. Run: git log --oneline --since="2026-03-21" | wc -l (count commits)
2. Group commits by capability area (following existing CHANGELOG structure):
   - On-Disk Parsing (ext4/btrfs)
   - FUSE Mount and VFS Layer
   - MVCC Concurrency Engine
   - Self-Healing Repair Pipeline
   - Write Path (Allocation, Extents, Inodes, Directories, Xattrs)
   - Journal and WAL Recovery
   - CLI and Observability
   - Conformance Harness and Testing
   - Foundation Types and Error Handling
3. Write entries with commit links: [hexsha](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenfs/commit/hexsha)
4. Update header: "Period covered: 2026-02-09 through 2026-04-06"
5. Update total commit count

## Considerations
- Follow existing style: bullet points grouped by feature area, commit links in brackets
- Don't document individual test additions — summarize as "expanded test coverage for X"
- Include the tracing callsite cache fix as it's architecturally interesting
- Include the btrfs fallocate sector-alignment fix
- Include the per-core metrics test fix
- Don't document build/fmt/cosmetic commits

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All significant commits since 2026-03-21 documented
- [ ] Organized by capability area matching existing structure
- [ ] Commit links included and correct
- [ ] Period and total count updated in header","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:33.040113984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037607885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037145379Z","close_reason":"Updated CHANGELOG.md through 2026-04-06 with 134-post-cutoff commit coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-idp4","depends_on_id":"bd-g1ue","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:11.676458110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ii3nk","title":"CONFORMANCE: freeze ext4 collapse/insert fallocate parity","description":"Queue-empty porting-to-rust fallback. Ext4 FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE and FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE are implemented in ffs-core, but FEATURE_PARITY.md only calls out ZERO_RANGE and existing executable evidence is crate-local unit coverage. Add ffs-harness conformance coverage for collapse shifting/shrinking and insert hole/growth behavior, then update FEATURE_PARITY.md so the spec-first parity record reflects the implemented contracts.","notes":"Claimed by Codex for porting-to-rust queue-empty parity fallback. Agent Mail health is red due missing schema tables, so coordination is recorded in Beads only.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:37:31.044827411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:51:43.929803844Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:51:43.927852409Z","close_reason":"Fixed ext4 COLLAPSE_RANGE EOF-reach rejection to return EINVAL, added ffs-harness conformance coverage for collapse shift/shrink, EOF rejection without mutation, and insert hole/growth behavior, updated parity evidence, and validated with fmt, diff check, targeted tests, full conformance gate, check, clippy, and UBS review. UBS reports the existing conformance.rs panic/expect inventory.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ik16s","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_detect_filesystem target","description":"ffs_core::detect_filesystem (lib.rs:177) - create new fuzz target","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:47:26.614709142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:59:29Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of implemented bead bd-kbe35; fuzz_detect_filesystem target now exists.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -4590,7 +4590,8 @@ Tracked V1 btrfs scope (explicit):
 
 - metadata parsing + validation
 - single- and multi-device chunk mapping for supported RAID/DUP read paths
-- transparent decompression (ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD)
+- transparent decompression (ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD) with exact `ram_bytes`
+  decoded-length validation and malformed LZO frame rejection
 - subvolume mount, tree-log replay, and send-stream parsing
 - btrfs ioctl path lookup for `BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP`, including `treeid=0`
   mounted-root resolution and explicit tree-id path resolution through ROOT_TREE

@@ -53,7 +53,7 @@
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |
 | btrfs scrub parity | `fs/btrfs/scrub.c` | ✅ | Scrub validation and repair-path coverage are implemented in the current test matrix. |
-| btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD) | `fs/btrfs/compression.c` | ✅ | `btrfs_decompress()` in `ffs-core` with flate2 (ZLIB), lzokay-native (LZO), and zstd crates. Inline + regular extent decompression wired into read path. All 3 codecs supported. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies mounted-root `lookup` plus exact regular-extent reads for ZLIB, LZO, and ZSTD compressed files, including a cross-page read slice through the decompressed view. |
+| btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB/LZO/ZSTD) | `fs/btrfs/compression.c` | ✅ | `btrfs_decompress()` in `ffs-core` with flate2 (ZLIB), lzokay-native (LZO), and zstd crates. Inline + regular extent decompression wired into read path, with exact `ram_bytes` decoded-length validation and malformed LZO frame rejection. All 3 codecs supported. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies mounted-root `lookup` plus exact regular-extent reads for ZLIB, LZO, and ZSTD compressed files, including a cross-page read slice through the decompressed view. |
 | btrfs subvolume mount | `fs/btrfs/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext::subvol_objectid` configurable; `walk_btrfs_fs_tree` resolves the requested tree root. `BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP` now follows the requested `treeid` contract: `treeid=0` reports the mounted subvolume objectid, explicit tree ids walk their matching ROOT_ITEM fs tree, and root-object lookups return a NUL-terminated empty path. Harness coverage still proves the default root alias, and `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` now adds operator-path coverage for named `--subvol` and `--snapshot` selection through the mount open path, including mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior scoped to the selected tree. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now adds FUSE E2E coverage (`#[ignore]` requiring sudo) for subvolume/snapshot mount selection proving root scoping to selected tree and `NotFound` errors for missing subvolumes, plus a mounted-path `BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP` ioctl probe covering `treeid=0` root resolution, seeded workspace path lookup, runtime-created child lookup through the writable COW tree, and missing-object `ENOENT`. |
 | ext4 inline data read | `fs/ext4/inline.c` | ✅ | `read_ext4_inline_data()` reads from inode extent_bytes + system.data xattr. `INLINE_DATA` removed from rejected features. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies real `OpenFs::read` behavior for both pure-`i_block` inline payloads and `system.data` xattr continuation payloads, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path ordinary reads plus near-EOF short reads for both storage layouts. |
 | ext4 indirect block addressing | `fs/ext4/inode.c` | ✅ | `resolve_indirect_block()` handles direct + single/double/triple indirect pointers. `EXTENTS` no longer required at mount. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies single/double indirect block resolution and I/O boundaries for non-extent inodes. |
